'''Nathan Mason''' (born 8 September 1993) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[2017 RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]], and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] and [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="hg">{{cite web|url=http://www.giantsrl.com/news/article/1956/giants-to-boast-biggest-pack|title=Giants to Boast Biggest Pack|website=Huddersfield Giants|access-date=1 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713134917/http://www.giantsrl.com/news/article/1956/giants-to-boast-biggest-pack|archive-date=13 July 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> Mason also played for the [[London Broncos]] in the Super League, and spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[Betfred Championship|second tier]].

==Background==
Nathan Mason was born in [[Oldham]], [[Greater Manchester, England]].

==Career==
In October 2017 he joined the [[Leigh Centurions]] on a one-year loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/blog/2017/10/23/nathan-mason-joins-leigh-centurions|title=Nathan Mason Joins Leigh Centurions|publisher=Leigh Centurions |date=23 October 2017|accessdate=7 January 2018}}</ref>

In 2019 he helped the Eagles to win the inaugural [[RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] as they defeated the [[Widnes Vikings]] 36–18 in the final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49458365|title=Sheffield Eagles beat Widnes Vikings 36–18 to win inaugural 1895 Cup|publisher=BBC Sport|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 September 2019}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 1 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 18-month deal<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=1 Jul 2024|accessdate=15 Dec 2024|author=Nathan Atkinson|title=Bradford Bulls get forward Nathan Mason back from Australia|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24423652.bradford-bulls-get-forward-nathan-mason-back-australia/}}</ref>

On 20 October 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Bradford Bulls]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League trio among quintet released by Bradford Bulls following promotion|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-trio-among-five-released-by-bradford-bulls-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>
